text: I feel disoriented—both by the sharp, reproachful words echoing in my mind and by the physical tilt of the rooms around me. The external world seems to mirror my internal turbulence, all bright lights and harsh reflections. Yet, as I stand in front of the display about confusion—a celebration of uncertainty—I sense an odd comfort. Maybe meaning isn’t found in moving forward or looking back, but in pausing amidst chaos to acknowledge its truth, if just for a moment.
